solution: 
```python
class ExamRoom:

    def __init__(self, n: int):
        self.n = n
        self.seats = set()

    def seat(self) -> int:
        if not self.seats:
            self.seats.add(0)
            return 0
        max_dist = 0
        best_seat = 0
        prev = -1
        for curr in sorted(self.seats):
            if prev == -1:
                dist = curr
            else:
                dist = (curr - prev) // 2
            if dist > max_dist:
                max_dist = dist
                best_seat = prev + dist if prev != -1 else curr
            prev = curr
        if (self.n - 1 - prev) > max_dist:
            best_seat = self.n - 1
        self.seats.add(best_seat)
        return best_seat

    def leave(self, p: int) -> None:
        if p in self.seats:
            self.seats.remove(p)
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(k log k) where k is the number of occupied seats
space complexity: O(k) where k is the number of occupied seats
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses a set to track occupied seats. When a student sits, it calculates the best seat by checking distances between existing occupied seats and the ends. The best seat is chosen to maximize the distance to the closest person. The leave method simply removes the seat from the set. The algorithm is efficient as it only processes occupied seats and avoids unnecessary computations.>
